comment.body: When Charm is negative, an option’s delta decreases as time passes, requiring market makers with a net negative Charm to buy the underlying asset to adjust their delta exposure. Conversely, positive Charm results in delta increasing over time, prompting market makers to sell the underlying to maintain their hedge -from optiondepth education post.
comment.raw_body: When Charm is negative, an option’s delta decreases as time passes, requiring market makers with a net negative Charm to buy the underlying asset to adjust their delta exposure. Conversely, positive Charm results in delta increasing over time, prompting market makers to sell the underlying to maintain their hedge -from optiondepth education post.

comment.body:
how do we determine if selling or buying from MM is suppressive or supportive in this case? I think MM is buying doesn't necessary means that is supportive nor selling does mean suppressive. In both case of course they provide more sellside or buyside but also more liquidity right. I'm a little confused
it's different than the case MM hedge gamma where they have two options depending on price fall or rise and they supprese the move when they are hedging against the price movement

comment.body: I think it’s hard for me because I understand the concept of MM essentially always playing both sides to remain delta neutral but reconciling that with price action and how to leverage gamma/almas notes/levels to make a trade is kind of overwhelming rn. I feel I was more effective and definitely profitable when only considering vix relative movement and trading with gamma targets as support/resistance even tho I know now that’s not accurate lol

comment.body:
I feel for you @Moc 

I think you can be an effective trader without knowing 100% of everything. Maybe inventory the things you have learned over time to be reliable truths. When you’ve defined your known knowns, then you can define your known unknowns after. 

And in the end price levels are what it’s all about and if you’re just level oriented you can generate profits. Definitely keep things simple “stupid” :)

comment.body: Generally that’s true for me too. I try to hold to target be it 10-15 pts or more ag best on runners but 3/4 on first trim to secure profit etc. not much of a scalper but lately there’s reversals or more chop etc so I’ve been caught holding too long even on shorter trades.
